confusion_points.description: After clicking 'Create account', a small spinning circle appeared next to the button text. I was not sure if this meant the page was loading, something had gone wrong, or I needed to do something else. I had to wait and take another look to confirm it was just processing.

suggestions: When the form is processing after clicking 'Create account', show a clear text message such as 'Creating your account, please wait...' instead of just a small spinning icon, so older users who may not recognize a spinner icon understand what is happening.

Consider adding a visible confirmation message like 'Account created successfully! Welcome, Margaret!' when the registration completes, rather than silently redirecting to Account Settings, so users know for certain that their registration worked.

The placeholder text in the Email field says 'you@organization.org', which implies this site is only for organizations or nonprofits. A regular personal email user might feel unsure whether their personal email address is acceptable. A note clarifying that personal emails are fine would be helpful.
reasoning:
Oh my, the page has changed! I can now see a page called "Account Settings" and at the top it shows my name "Margaret" in the corner, which means the account was created successfully! I can also see it says "Member since 3/31/2026" which tells me my new account is set up. The form shows my email address and my name — Margaret Henderson — are all there correctly.

The account has been created successfully. I am now logged in to GatherGood as Margaret Henderson.
